name: Delete User Account Load Test (Soft Delete via PATCH)
description: Test the /api/v1/users/{user_id} endpoint under 50 concurrent virtual users performing PATCH operations to soft-delete (deactivate) user accounts for 5 minutes, validating max response time, success rate, and data integrity requirements for the user deactivation operation.
corresponding NFR: The /api/v1/users/{user_id} endpoint must maintain a maximum response time of ≤ 500 ms, a success rate of ≥ 98%, and data integrity (deactivated users must not appear in the active users listing) under 50 concurrent PATCH deactivation operations.
preconditions: The application is deployed and fully accessible in a stable test environment.
A pool of at least 50 unique test user accounts in active status is pre-created and restored from a snapshot before the test run.
None of the test users have pending transactions or dependencies that would block deactivation.
Valid Bearer authentication tokens are pre-generated for each virtual user and included in the Authorization header with a TTL exceeding the total test duration.
Each virtual user is mapped to a distinct user_id to prevent duplicate deactivation attempts on the same resource.
A data integrity verification step is scripted to confirm deactivated users do not appear in the active users listing post-execution.

input data.path_parameter: user_id (drawn from pre-seeded active user pool)

metrics.unit: % of deactivated users absent from active users listing

notes: Ramp-up duration of 30 s and steady-state duration of 300 s are proposed defaults. Because this is a destructive (non-idempotent) operation, the pre-seeded user pool must be fully restored between test iterations to maintain a valid active-user supply. Data integrity validation requires a post-test verification query or GET request confirming deactivated users are absent from the active users listing — this must be implemented as a post-test assertion in the test script. Bearer tokens must be pre-generated with a TTL greater than total test duration (ramp-up + steady-state = 330 s minimum).
